concepts.name: canonical variates
concepts.description:
This is a generalized eigenvalue problem, with a given by the largest
eigenvalue of W−1B. It is not hard to show (Exercise 4.1) that the optimal
a1 is identical to v1 deﬁned above. Similarly one can ﬁnd the next direction
a2, orthogonal in W to a1, such that aT
2 Ba2/aT
2 Wa2 is maximized; the
solution is a2 = v2, and so on. The aℓare referred to as discriminant
coordinates, not to be confused with discriminant functions. They are also
referred to as canonical variates, since an alternative derivation of these
results is through a canonical correlation analysis of the indicator response
matrix Y on the predictor matrix X. This line is pursued in Section 12.5.
To summarize the developments so far:

concepts.name: adjusted response
concepts.description:
sometimes known as the adjusted response. These equations get solved re-
peatedly, since at each iteration p changes, and hence so does W and z.
This algorithm is referred to as iteratively reweighted least squares or IRLS,
since each iteration solves the weighted least squares problem:

concepts.name: support vector machine
concepts.description:
separation through over-ﬁtting. In Chapter 12 we discuss a more attractive
alternative known as the support vector machine, which allows for overlap,
but minimizes a measure of the extent of this overlap.

concepts.name: warm start
concepts.description: An initialization technique where the solution from a previous optimization problem is used as the starting point for a new similar problem, commonly used in pathwise optimization algorithms.

concepts.name: cholesky decomposition
concepts.description: A matrix decomposition that factors a positive-definite matrix into the product of a lower triangular matrix and its transpose, used in least squares fitting.

concepts.name: indicator response matrix
concepts.description: A matrix representation of categorical responses where each class is coded as a separate binary indicator variable, forming an N × K matrix for K classes.

concepts.name: masking in classification
concepts.description: A phenomenon in linear regression for classification where one class becomes dominated by others and is never predicted, especially when K ≥ 3 classes are arranged in certain configurations.

concepts.name: class-conditional density
concepts.description: The probability density function of the input features X conditional on belonging to a specific class k, denoted fk(x).

concepts.name: linear discriminant function
concepts.description: A linear function δk(x) = x^T Σ^(-1)μk - (1/2)μk^T Σ^(-1)μk + log πk used in LDA to classify observations by finding the maximum discriminant value.

concepts.name: pooled covariance matrix
concepts.description: A weighted average of the within-class covariance matrices used in LDA when assuming a common covariance across all classes.

concepts.name: quadratic discriminant function
concepts.description: A discriminant function that includes quadratic terms in x, arising when different classes have different covariance matrices in Gaussian classification.

concepts.name: sphering the data
concepts.description: A transformation that rescales data so that its covariance matrix becomes the identity matrix, often done using X* ← D^(-1/2) U^T X where Σ = UDU^T.

concepts.name: reduced-rank lda
concepts.description: A dimension reduction technique in LDA that projects data onto a lower-dimensional subspace spanned by the class centroids, preserving classification information while enabling visualization.

concepts.name: fisher's linear discriminant
concepts.description: A method that finds linear combinations of features that maximize the ratio of between-class variance to within-class variance, providing optimal low-dimensional projections for classification.

concepts.name: rayleigh quotient
concepts.description: The ratio a^T Ba / a^T Wa used in Fisher's discriminant analysis, measuring the between-class to within-class variance ratio for a linear combination.

concepts.name: discriminant coordinates
concepts.description: The vectors aℓ that maximize the Rayleigh quotient successively, providing optimal directions for discriminating between classes.

concepts.name: between-class covariance
concepts.description: The covariance matrix B measuring the spread of class means around the overall mean, used in Fisher's discriminant analysis.

concepts.name: within-class covariance
concepts.description: The pooled covariance matrix W measuring the variation of observations within each class around their class means.

concepts.name: logit transformation
concepts.description: A monotone transformation log[p/(1−p)] that maps probabilities from [0,1] to the real line, used in logistic regression to model posterior probabilities.

concepts.name: log-odds
concepts.description: The logarithm of the odds ratio, equal to the logit transformation, representing the linear component in logistic regression models.

concepts.name: score equations
concepts.description: The system of equations formed by setting the derivatives of the log-likelihood to zero, solved to find maximum likelihood estimates.

concepts.name: hessian matrix
concepts.description: The matrix of second derivatives of a function, used in Newton-Raphson optimization to determine the curvature and update direction.

concepts.name: iteratively reweighted least squares
concepts.description: An iterative algorithm (IRLS) for fitting generalized linear models like logistic regression, where each iteration solves a weighted least squares problem with data-dependent weights.

concepts.name: adjusted response in logistic regression
concepts.description: The linearized response z = Xβ + W^(-1)(y − p) used in the IRLS algorithm for logistic regression, representing a working response.

concepts.name: wald test
concepts.description: A hypothesis test for whether a coefficient is zero based on the ratio of the estimate to its standard error (Z score), used for variable selection in logistic regression.

concepts.name: residual deviance
concepts.description: Minus twice the log-likelihood of a fitted model, used to measure model fit and compare nested models in generalized linear models.

concepts.name: pearson chi-square statistic
concepts.description: A goodness-of-fit statistic equal to the weighted residual sum-of-squares in logistic regression, measuring the discrepancy between observed and fitted values.

concepts.name: rao score test
concepts.description: A hypothesis test for including a term in a model based on the score function at the current parameter estimates, not requiring iterative fitting.

concepts.name: l1 regularized logistic regression
concepts.description: Logistic regression with an L1 penalty on the coefficients, promoting sparsity and automatic variable selection.

concepts.name: conditional likelihood
concepts.description: The likelihood of the response given the predictors, Pr(G|X), used in logistic regression which ignores the marginal distribution of X.

concepts.name: joint likelihood in lda
concepts.description: The full likelihood Pr(X,G) = Pr(X)Pr(G|X) used in LDA, incorporating both the marginal distribution of X and the class posteriors.

concepts.name: separating hyperplane
concepts.description: A hyperplane that perfectly separates two classes in feature space, with all points of one class on one side and all points of the other class on the opposite side.

concepts.name: perceptron
concepts.description: A linear classifier that computes a linear combination of input features and returns the sign, developed by Rosenblatt in the 1950s as a foundation for neural networks.

concepts.name: affine set
concepts.description: A set defined by a linear equation f(x) = β₀ + β^T x = 0, generalizing hyperplanes to not necessarily pass through the origin.

concepts.name: signed distance to hyperplane
concepts.description: The perpendicular distance from a point to a hyperplane, with sign indicating which side of the hyperplane the point lies on, given by f(x)/||β||.

concepts.name: perceptron learning algorithm
concepts.description: An algorithm that finds a separating hyperplane by minimizing the distance of misclassified points to the decision boundary using stochastic gradient descent.

concepts.name: stochastic gradient descent
concepts.description: An optimization method that updates parameters after each observation rather than computing gradients over the entire dataset, used in the perceptron algorithm.

concepts.name: lagrange primal function
concepts.description: The objective function augmented with Lagrange multipliers for constraints, minimized to solve constrained optimization problems.

concepts.name: support points in separating hyperplanes
concepts.description: Training points that lie exactly on the margin boundary (where αᵢ > 0), which define the solution vector β in the optimal separating hyperplane.

concepts.name: piecewise polynomial
concepts.description: A function obtained by dividing the domain into contiguous intervals and representing the function by a separate polynomial in each interval.

concepts.name: knots in splines
concepts.description: The boundary points ξ₁, ξ₂, ... where the piecewise polynomial segments join, defining the intervals in piecewise polynomial regression.

concepts.name: continuity constraints in splines
concepts.description: Linear constraints on parameters ensuring that piecewise polynomial segments connect smoothly at knots, reducing the effective number of parameters.

concepts.name: regression splines
concepts.description:
It is claimed that cubic splines are the lowest-order spline for which the
knot-discontinuity is not visible to the human eye. There is seldom any
good reason to go beyond cubic-splines, unless one is interested in smooth
derivatives. In practice the most widely used orders are M = 1, 2 and 4.
These ﬁxed-knot splines are also known as regression splines. One needs
to select the order of the spline, the number of knots and their placement.
One simple approach is to parameterize a family of splines by the number
of basis functions or degrees of freedom, and have the observations xi de-
termine the positions of the knots. For example, the expression bs(x,df=7)
in R generates a basis matrix of cubic-spline functions evaluated at the N
observations in x, with the 7−3 = 41 interior knots at the appropriate per-
centiles of x (20, 40, 60 and 80th.) One can be more explicit, however; bs(x,
degree=1, knots = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6)) generates a basis for linear splines,
with three interior knots, and returns an N × 4 matrix.
Since the space of spline functions of a particular order and knot sequence
is a vector space, there are many equivalent bases for representing them
(just as there are for ordinary polynomials.) While the truncated power
basis is conceptually simple, it is not too attractive numerically: powers of
large numbers can lead to severe rounding problems. The B-spline basis,
described in the Appendix to this chapter, allows for eﬃcient computations
even when the number of knots K is large.

concepts.name: smoother matrix
concepts.description:
Again the ﬁt is linear in y, and the ﬁnite linear operator Sλ is known as
the smoother matrix. One consequence of this linearity is that the recipe
for producing ˆf from y does not depend on y itself; Sλ depends only on
the xi and λ.
Linear operators are familiar in more traditional least squares ﬁtting as
well. Suppose Bξ is a N × M matrix of M cubic-spline basis functions
evaluated at the N training points xi, with knot sequence ξ, and M ≪N.
Then the vector of ﬁtted spline values is given by

concepts.name: penalty matrix
concepts.description:
K is known as the penalty matrix, and indeed a quadratic form in K has
a representation in terms of a weighted sum of squared (divided) second
diﬀerences. The eigen-decomposition of Sλ is

concepts.name: shrinking smoothers
concepts.description:
either left alone, or shrunk to zero—that is, a projection matrix such
as Hξ above has M eigenvalues equal to 1, and the rest are 0. For
this reason smoothing splines are referred to as shrinking smoothers,
while regression splines are projection smoothers (see Figure 3.17 on
page 80).

concepts.name: B-splines
concepts.description:
Before we can get started, we need to augment the knot sequence deﬁned
in Section 5.2. Let ξ0 < ξ1 and ξK < ξK+1 be two boundary knots, which
typically deﬁne the domain over which we wish to evaluate our spline. We
now deﬁne the augmented knot sequence τ such that

concepts.name: Haar basis functions
concepts.description: for i = 1, . . . , K + 2M −1. These are also known as Haar basis functions.

concepts.name: roughness penalty
concepts.description: A penalty term based on the integrated squared second derivative, penalizing curvature in the function.

concepts.name: feature extraction
concepts.description: Preprocessing high-dimensional features through transformation (linear or nonlinear) before applying a learning algorithm.

concepts.name: linear smoother
concepts.description: A fitting method where fitted values are a linear function of responses, characterized by a smoother matrix independent of y.

concepts.name: hat matrix
concepts.description: The matrix H = X(XT X)^-1 XT that puts the hat on y by computing the orthogonal projection onto the column space of X.

concepts.name: effective degrees of freedom
concepts.description: For ridge regression, df(lambda) = sum of d_j^2/(d_j^2 + lambda), measuring the number of parameters effectively used in a regularized fit.

concepts.name: truncated power basis
concepts.description: A basis for representing continuous piecewise linear functions using terms like (x − ξ)₊ that are zero below the knot and linear above.

concepts.name: natural cubic spline
concepts.description: A cubic spline with the additional constraint that the function is linear beyond the boundary knots, reducing boundary variance.

concepts.name: order-M spline
concepts.description: A piecewise-polynomial of order M with continuous derivatives up to order M-2 at the knots.

concepts.name: cubic spline
concepts.description: An order-4 spline with continuous first and second derivatives at knots, the lowest order where knot discontinuity is invisible to the human eye.

concepts.name: pointwise variance
concepts.description: The variance of a fitted function at a specific point, measuring uncertainty in the fit at that location.

concepts.name: backward stepwise deletion
concepts.description: A model selection procedure that iteratively removes terms from a model to minimize an information criterion while preserving group structure.

concepts.name: functional modeling
concepts.description: Using splines to reduce rather than increase flexibility by constraining coefficients to vary smoothly as functions.

concepts.name: contrast functional
concepts.description: A function of coefficients that highlights regions where classes differ, having appreciable values where features discriminate between classes.

concepts.name: filtered features
concepts.description: Transformed features x* derived from raw features x through basis expansion or other preprocessing.

concepts.name: smoothing spline
concepts.description: A spline that minimizes penalized RSS with a knot at every unique x value, controlling complexity via a roughness penalty rather than knot selection.

concepts.name: penalized residual sum of squares
concepts.description: An objective function balancing data fit against function roughness, with a smoothing parameter controlling the tradeoff.

concepts.name: Sobolev space
concepts.description: An infinite-dimensional function space of functions with bounded derivatives, used as the domain for smoothing spline optimization.

concepts.name: generalized ridge regression
concepts.description: Ridge regression with a general penalty matrix rather than identity, arising from smoothing spline formulation.

concepts.name: Reinsch form
concepts.description: An alternative representation Sλ = (I + λK)⁻¹ separating the smoothing parameter from the penalty structure.

concepts.name: projection smoothers
concepts.description: Smoothers with idempotent smoother matrices having eigenvalues of exactly 1 or 0, selecting basis functions without shrinkage.

concepts.name: Demmler-Reinsch basis
concepts.description: The eigenvector basis of the smoother matrix, providing a representation where the penalty is diagonal.

concepts.name: integrated squared prediction error
concepts.description: Expected prediction error averaged over both training samples and prediction points, combining bias and variance.

concepts.name: nonparametric logistic regression
concepts.description: Logistic regression with a smooth nonparametric function replacing the linear predictor, fit via penalized log-likelihood.

concepts.name: penalized log-likelihood
concepts.description: A log-likelihood criterion augmented with a roughness penalty for fitting smooth nonparametric models.

concepts.name: weighted smoothing spline
concepts.description: A smoothing spline fit with observation-specific weights, arising in iterative algorithms like Newton-Raphson for GLMs.

concepts.name: tensor product basis
concepts.description: A multidimensional basis formed by taking products of one-dimensional basis functions for each coordinate.

concepts.name: wavelet smoothing
concepts.description: A smoothing method using wavelet basis functions, effective for functions with discontinuities and sharp features.

concepts.name: wavelet transform
concepts.description: A transformation decomposing signals into wavelet coefficients at different scales and locations.

concepts.name: AIC statistic
concepts.description: Akaike Information Criterion - a model selection criterion balancing fit and complexity, used for term selection in stepwise procedures.

concepts.name: B-spline basis
concepts.description: A numerically stable basis for representing splines, allowing efficient computation even with many knots.

concepts.name: boundary knots
concepts.description: The extreme knots defining the domain boundaries where spline behavior can become erratic.

concepts.name: interior knots
concepts.description: Knots placed within the data range, controlling spline flexibility in regions with data.

concepts.name: idempotent matrix
concepts.description: A matrix A satisfying A² = A, characteristic of projection operators in linear algebra.

concepts.name: positive semidefinite matrix
concepts.description: A symmetric matrix with non-negative eigenvalues, ensuring non-negative quadratic forms.

concepts.name: eigendecomposition
concepts.description: The decomposition of a matrix into eigenvectors and eigenvalues, revealing the structure of linear operators.

concepts.name: MARS
concepts.description: Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines - a greedy algorithm for adaptive knot placement in regression splines.

concepts.name: equivalent kernel
concepts.description:
yi (the li(x0) do not involve y). These weights li(x0) combine the weight-
ing kernel Kλ(x0, ·) and the least squares operations, and are sometimes
referred to as the equivalent kernel. Figure 6.4 illustrates the eﬀect of lo-
cal linear regression on the equivalent kernel. Historically, the bias in the
Nadaraya–Watson and other local average kernel methods were corrected
by modifying the kernel. These modiﬁcations were based on theoretical
asymptotic mean-square-error considerations, and besides being tedious to
implement, are only approximate for ﬁnite sample sizes. Local linear re-
gression automatically modiﬁes the kernel to correct the bias exactly to
ﬁrst order, a phenomenon dubbed as automatic kernel carpentry. Consider
the following expansion for E ˆf(x0), using the linearity of local regression
and a series expansion of the true function f around x0,

concepts.name: expected prediction error
concepts.description: The expected test error averaging over both the randomness in test data and the randomness in the training set, denoted Err = E_T E_{X⁰,Y⁰}[L(Y⁰, f̂(X⁰)|T)].

concepts.name: training error
concepts.description: The average loss over the training sample, which consistently decreases with model complexity and is not a good estimate of test error because it is overly optimistic.

concepts.name: local linear regression
concepts.description: A locally weighted regression technique that fits straight lines rather than constants locally by solving weighted least squares problems at each target point, which removes boundary bias exactly to first order.

concepts.name: bias-variance decomposition
concepts.description: The decomposition of expected prediction error into irreducible error σ²_ε, squared bias [Ef̂(x₀)-f(x₀)]², and variance E[f̂(x₀)-Ef̂(x₀)]². More complex models have lower bias but higher variance.

concepts.name: in-sample error
concepts.description: The expected prediction error for N new response values at each of the training input points, denoted Err_in = (1/N)Σᵢ E_Y⁰[L(Yᵢ⁰, f̂(xᵢ))|T], used to measure optimism of training error.

concepts.name: cp statistic
concepts.description: An estimate of expected prediction error for linear models under squared error loss, equal to training error plus (2d/N)σ̂²_ε where d is the number of parameters, adjusting for optimism.

concepts.name: automatic kernel carpentry
concepts.description: The phenomenon whereby local linear regression automatically modifies the kernel to correct the bias exactly to first order, without requiring manual kernel modifications based on asymptotic theory.

concepts.name: local polynomial regression
concepts.description: An extension of local linear regression that fits polynomials of degree d locally, providing bias reduction at the cost of increased variance. Local quadratic regression (d=2) can correct curvature bias in the interior of the domain.

concepts.name: trimming the hills and filling the valleys
concepts.description: A bias phenomenon in local linear fits where the estimator tends to be biased in regions of curvature of the true function, underestimating peaks and overestimating valleys.

concepts.name: nadaraya-watson kernel smoother
concepts.description: A kernel smoothing method that fits a constant locally with weights supplied by a kernel, which suffers from boundary bias that can be corrected by local linear regression.

concepts.name: varying coefficient models
concepts.description: A class of structured regression models where the model is conditionally linear for some predictors, but the coefficients are allowed to vary smoothly as a function of other covariates, fit using locally weighted least squares.

concepts.name: local likelihood
concepts.description: A method that generalizes local regression by using likelihood-based models fit locally, allowing parametric models to be made locally flexible. Any parametric model can be made local if the fitting method accommodates observation weights.

concepts.name: parzen density estimate
concepts.description: A smooth kernel density estimator that counts observations close to a point with weights that decrease with distance, using a kernel such as the Gaussian. It is the convolution of the empirical distribution with a smoothing kernel.

concepts.name: naive bayes classifier
concepts.description: A classification method that assumes features are independent given the class, dramatically simplifying density estimation by estimating only univariate marginal densities. Despite strong assumptions, it often outperforms more sophisticated alternatives.

concepts.name: radial basis functions
concepts.description: A method that treats kernel functions as basis functions indexed by location parameters ξⱼ and scale parameters λⱼ, combining ideas from kernel methods and basis function expansions to model f(x) = Σⱼ βⱼ D(||x-ξⱼ||/λⱼ).

concepts.name: rbf network
concepts.description: A neural network alternative where all parameters (locations ξⱼ, scales λⱼ, and coefficients βⱼ) are optimized jointly via sum-of-squares minimization, though the criterion is nonconvex with multiple local minima.

concepts.name: renormalized radial basis functions
concepts.description: A modification of radial basis functions that normalizes each basis function by the sum of all basis functions, avoiding holes (regions where no kernel has appreciable support) when using constant scale parameters.

concepts.name: gaussian mixture model
concepts.description: A density estimation method representing a distribution as a weighted sum of Gaussian densities with mixing proportions αₘ, means μₘ, and covariance matrices Σₘ, typically fit by maximum likelihood using the EM algorithm.

concepts.name: memory-based methods
concepts.description: Learning methods where the model is the entire training data set and fitting is done at evaluation or prediction time, leading to O(N) computational cost per evaluation but making them infeasible for real-time applications.

concepts.name: model selection
concepts.description: The task of estimating the performance of different models in order to choose the best one, typically by estimating prediction error for various tuning parameters.

concepts.name: model assessment
concepts.description: The task of estimating the prediction error (generalization error) of a final chosen model on new data, ideally using a held-out test set kept in a vault.

concepts.name: model bias
concepts.description: The error between the best-fitting model in a restricted class and the true function, representing limitations of the model family itself rather than estimation error.

concepts.name: optimism
concepts.description: The difference between in-sample error and training error, representing how much training error underestimates true error. For many loss functions, optimism = (2/N)Σᵢ Cov(ŷᵢ, yᵢ).

concepts.name: effective number of parameters
concepts.description: For linear fitting methods ŷ = Sy, defined as trace(S), generalizing the concept of number of parameters to regularized models. Replaces d in the Cₚ statistic for non-standard fits.

concepts.name: vapnik-chervonenkis dimension
concepts.description: A measure of model complexity defined as the largest number of points (in some configuration) that can be shattered by members of a function class. A set of points is shattered if the class can perfectly separate any binary labeling of them.

concepts.name: structural risk minimization
concepts.description: Vapnik's approach to model selection that fits a nested sequence of models of increasing VC dimensions and chooses the model minimizing an upper bound on test error based on VC theory.

concepts.name: k-fold cross-validation
concepts.description: A method for estimating prediction error by splitting data into K parts, fitting the model to K-1 parts and testing on the remaining part, then averaging the K prediction errors. Typical choices are K=5 or K=10.

concepts.name: one-standard-error rule
concepts.description: A model selection heuristic used with cross-validation that chooses the most parsimonious model whose error is no more than one standard error above the error of the best model.

concepts.name: epanechnikov kernel
concepts.description: A compact support kernel function used in local regression, flatter on top like nearest-neighbor box kernels and requiring careful choice of window size.

concepts.name: tri-cube kernel
concepts.description: A popular compact support kernel function that is flatter on top than the Epanechnikov kernel and is differentiable at the boundary of its support, used in local regression.

concepts.name: boundary bias in kernel methods
concepts.description: A bias problem in kernel smoothing where locally-weighted averages are badly biased on the boundaries of the domain because of kernel asymmetry. Local linear regression corrects this bias exactly to first order.

concepts.name: curse of dimensionality in local regression
concepts.description: The phenomenon that in high dimensions, it becomes impossible to simultaneously maintain localness (low bias) and a sizable neighborhood sample (low variance) without exponential sample size growth. The fraction of boundary points approaches one as dimension grows.

concepts.name: anova decomposition in regression
concepts.description: A decomposition of the regression function into main effects, two-way interactions, and higher-order interactions: f(X) = α + Σⱼ gⱼ(Xⱼ) + Σⱼ<ₖ gⱼₖ(Xⱼ,Xₖ) + ···, used to introduce structure by eliminating higher-order terms.

concepts.name: trellis display
concepts.description: A visualization method showing the response as a function of one variable, conditioned on ranges of other variables, implemented as a grid of panels each showing a slice of the data.

concepts.name: structured kernels
concepts.description: Kernels using a positive semidefinite matrix A to weight different coordinates: Kλ,A(x₀,x) = D(||(x-x₀)ᵀA||/λ), allowing individual predictors to be downgraded or entire directions to be omitted.

concepts.name: instantaneous prefix code
concepts.description: A coding scheme where no code is the prefix of any other, allowing the receiver to know exactly when a message has been completely sent without requiring delimiters.

concepts.name: learning curve
concepts.description: A plot of prediction performance (e.g., 1-Err) versus training set size N, showing how classifier performance improves with more data and used to assess cross-validation bias.
